mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or remains at very low levels, discount rates stay compressed and equity valuation multiples expand, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month. This signal is falsified if low or declining FEDFUNDS is followed by systematically negative 20-day SPX returns.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods with FEDFUNDS declining over the prior 3 months, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a short-run threshold signals worsening household income risk and weaker cyclical demand, which should pressure S&P 500 prices lower over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when unemployment is rising, not merely elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes of UNRATE increases above 0.3pp in 3 months are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in HOUST and RSAFS indicates a broadening demand upswing across housing and retail activity, which should support earnings expectations and push SPX higher over the next month. The signal fails when one series improves but the other deteriorates.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising HOUST and rising RSAFS does not outperform either series alone in forecasting 20-day SPX returns, or if hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO after a weak reading signals a rebound in industrial output and operating leverage for cyclicals, which should raise S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The mechanism is strongest when production acceleration is broad-based rather than temporary.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month rising INDPRO episodes are followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in a majority of cases or if the rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03 for 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS accelerates while UNRATE falls, labor income growth and job security strengthen consumer spending expectations, which should lift SPX over the next 20 trading days. The joint labor improvement matters more than either series separately.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down condition fails to deliver positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of observations or adds no incremental predictive power versus single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation surge combined with a low FEDFUNDS rate implies real policy rates are deeply negative, supporting risk assets until the market prices in tightening. This should be positive for SPX only in the initial inflation shock phase and should fail once rate hikes begin to accelerate.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered high-inflation/low-rate periods are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the sign flips after tightening begins.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable UNRATE in a mature expansion supports durable earnings and suppresses recession risk, which should produce positive SPX returns over the next quarter. The signal is invalid if unemployment is already turning higher sharply.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 5.5% and not rising by more than 0.1pp over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if low-stable UNRATE episodes are followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 45% of cases or if downturns in UNRATE lead the signal to reverse.

mechanism_narrative: A GDP-consumption confirmation pattern, where GDPC1 is firm and RSAFS is accelerating, indicates real final demand is holding up and corporate revenue growth should support the index over the next month. The pattern fails when GDP strength is not matched by retail sales strength.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1-RSAFS confirmation does not outperform GDPC1 alone for 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal's hit rate remains below 55% in walk-forward testing.
